argument_substitution.mechanism: {{args}} placeholder injects all user-provided arguments; processed before shell injection (!{...}) and other substitutions; default argument handling collapses newlines

builtin_commands.mechanism: built-in slash commands include /commands reload (reload command files without restart) and /help; user-defined commands supplement but do not replace built-ins

providerExtensions.name: TOML command file format
providerExtensions.summary: Commands are .toml files with a required prompt field and an optional one-line description; no other fields are documented in v1.

providerExtensions.name: Namespace via subdirectory path
providerExtensions.summary: Path separators in the file path relative to commands/ become colons in the command name (e.g., git/commit.toml → /git:commit).

providerExtensions.name: {{args}} argument injection placeholder
providerExtensions.summary: {{args}} in prompts is replaced with user-supplied text — raw in the body, shell-escaped inside !{...} blocks; absent placeholder appends args after two newlines.

providerExtensions.name: Shell command injection with !{...}
providerExtensions.summary: !{...} executes a shell command and injects its stdout into the prompt, with user confirmation and automatic escaping of {{args}}.

providerExtensions.name: File content injection with @{...}
providerExtensions.summary: @{...} inlines file content or directory listings (text, images, PDFs, audio, video) into the prompt, respecting .gitignore and .geminiignore.

providerExtensions.name: Project commands override user commands
providerExtensions.summary: When the same command name exists in both project and user commands directories, the project version always wins.

providerExtensions.name: /commands reload for live updates
providerExtensions.summary: /commands reload picks up newly created or modified .toml files without restarting the CLI for iterative development.
